evals.prompt: I've added all my Figma URLs to figma-paths.yaml and the dev server is running. How do I extract the colors and fonts from Figma into the codebase?
evals.expected_output: Triggers /extractify-setup command. Runs the 6-step token extraction wizard: colors → typography → grid → icons → buttons → form elements.

assertions.description: Reads _docs/figma-paths.yaml to find the Figma URLs

evals.prompt: Build me the hero banner component from this Figma URL: https://www.figma.com/design/ABC123/MyProject?node-id=5-22
evals.expected_output: Triggers /extractify-new-component command. Spawns subagents for build → compliance → visual review phases. Produces a typed React component with preview page.

assertions.description: Generated component uses from next/image, not bare

evals.prompt: I've built a bunch of components over the past few weeks. Can you check if they all follow the project rules correctly?
evals.expected_output: Triggers /extractify-audit command. Scans all components in src/components/ and produces a compliance report at .audit/report.md.

assertions.description: Lists all directories in src/components/ before auditing
Outputs a report with pass/fail per component

evals.prompt: My Figma file doesn't have a design system page yet — the designers just started building screens. Can you figure out what colors and fonts they're using?
evals.expected_output: Triggers /extractify-discover command. Scans all pages of the Figma file, extracts tokens from actual designs, and produces discovered-tokens.yaml.

assertions.description: Asks for the Figma file URL if not provided

evals.prompt: The hero component is done and approved. I want the designers to see the actual React props when they inspect it in Figma Dev Mode.
evals.expected_output: Triggers /extractify-code-connect command. Maps the hero component to its Figma node and creates a .figma-connect.ts file.

assertions.description: Creates or describes creating a .figma-connect.ts file
